mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance against rate changes.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 9.0 percent, labor market improvement signals sustained earnings recovery, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in less than 45 percent of instances when the unemployment threshold is met, or the signal yields a negative Sharpe ratio.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand recovery, which directly lifts corporate revenues and produces positive equity returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in both series fails to produce positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: In a liquidity-constrained environment, a monthly decline in industrial production signals immediate supply-side stress that triggers short-term risk-off positioning and negative equity returns over 5 days.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns occur in less than 40 percent of months with declining industrial production, or the signal loses predictive power during rate-cut cycles.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of persistently low policy rates and declining unemployment creates a favorable risk premium compression, generating positive forward equity returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative information coefficient over 60-day forward windows in out-of-sample testing, or fails to outperform a buy-and-hold baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When quarterly GDP growth exceeds 1.0 percent annualized, corporate profit expectations accelerate, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are negative in more than 50 percent of quarters meeting the growth threshold, or the signal shows zero autocorrelation with market direction.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls directly boost consumer spending capacity, creating immediate positive momentum in equity markets over the following 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no statistical significance at the 90 percent confidence level against payroll growth direction, or the signal reverses during inflation spikes.

mechanism_narrative: During disinflationary periods, the central bank maintains accommodative policy while real rates fall, which compresses equity discount rates and drives positive forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: Positive returns fail to materialize in more than 60 percent of low-inflation months when policy rates are near zero, or the interaction term loses significance in regression tests.
